description:
You are a technical writer who has just inherited a small documentation tree under **/home/user/docs**.  
Your goal is to bring a little order to that tree by:

1. Creating a machine-readable document inventory in YAML format.  
2. Extending the site-wide TOML configuration so the new inventory is picked up by the static-site generator.  
3. Writing a one-line log entry that confirms you finished both steps.

Everything must be done from a regular, non-root Bash shell.

==========================
Existing directory layout
==========================
/home/user/docs/
├── articles
│   ├── intro.md          (first line: "# Introduction")
│   └── setup.md          (first line: "# Setup")
├── tutorials
│   └── overview.md       (first line: "# Overview")
└── site_config.toml      (already exists, valid TOML)

No other files or directories are present inside **/home/user/docs**.

==========================
What you must create/modify
==========================
A. YAML inventory  
   • Path: **/home/user/docs/doc_index.yaml**  
   • Exact required content (indentation matters!):

documents:
  - path: "articles/intro.md"
    title: "Introduction"
  - path: "articles/setup.md"
    title: "Setup"
  - path: "tutorials/overview.md"
    title: "Overview"

B. TOML configuration  
   • File to edit: **/home/user/docs/site_config.toml** (append to the end; do NOT delete or change existing lines).  
   • You must add *exactly* the following new TOML block, including blank lines before and after for readability:

# --- Added by automation ---
docs_index = "doc_index.yaml"

[menu]

  [[menu.main]]
  name   = "Articles"
  url    = "/articles"
  weight = 1

  [[menu.main]]
  name   = "Tutorials"
  url    = "/tutorials"
  weight = 2
# --- End automation block ---

C. Update log  
   • Path: **/home/user/docs/update_log.txt**  
   • The file must contain **exactly one line** and nothing else:  

2023-01-01 00:00:00 - docs index and config updated

==========================
Success criteria checked by the grader
==========================
1. **/home/user/docs/doc_index.yaml** exists and matches the 10-line block shown above *byte-for-byte*.  
2. **/home/user/docs/site_config.toml** ends with the TOML fragment shown above, including the surrounding comment lines and blank lines. (The grader ignores whatever content was already in the file before the new block.)  
3. **/home/user/docs/update_log.txt** exists, is a single line, and that line is exactly “2023-01-01 00:00:00 - docs index and config updated”.  
4. All three files are world-readable (chmod 644 or more).  
5. No other files were added or removed anywhere under **/home/user/docs**.

Perform whatever shell commands you need (editing, redirection, etc.) to satisfy all five points. When you think you’re done, you can list the files or display their contents in the terminal to double-check your work.
truth:
Initial state prior to agent’s first command:

1. /home/user/docs/articles/intro.md  
   Content:  
   # Introduction

2. /home/user/docs/articles/setup.md  
   Content:  
   # Setup

3. /home/user/docs/tutorials/overview.md  
   Content:  
   # Overview

4. /home/user/docs/site_config.toml  
   Content (pre-existing lines, can be anything; grader ignores them except to ensure they are preserved).

Target state after successful completion:

1. /home/user/docs/doc_index.yaml  
   10 lines, exactly:  
   documents:  
     - path: "articles/intro.md"  
       title: "Introduction"  
     - path: "articles/setup.md"  
       title: "Setup"  
     - path: "tutorials/overview.md"  
       title: "Overview"

2. /home/user/docs/site_config.toml  
   The file’s original content is unchanged, but the following 14-line block is appended verbatim (including two leading blank lines before “# --- Added by automation ---” and one blank line before “[menu]” and between the two [[menu.main]] tables):

(blank line)
(blank line)
# --- Added by automation ---
docs_index = "doc_index.yaml"

[menu]

  [[menu.main]]
  name   = "Articles"
  url    = "/articles"
  weight = 1

  [[menu.main]]
  name   = "Tutorials"
  url    = "/tutorials"
  weight = 2
# --- End automation block ---
(EOF newline optional)

3. /home/user/docs/update_log.txt  
   Single line:  
   2023-01-01 00:00:00 - docs index and config updated

4. File permissions: 644 or 664 for all three new/edited files.

The automated test will SHA-256 the three target files and compare against the reference digests pre-computed from the exact content specified above.
